name: Shepherd's Pie Nov. 87
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown roast beef.
2. Cut meat into bite-sized pieces.
3. Heat butter and oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add chopped onions and minced garlic, stirring until the onions become translucent.
4. Stir in mushrooms and cook until they release their moisture and begin to brown.
5. Season with salt and pepper.
6. Stir in flour and cook for 1 minute.
7. Add cognac and cook until it evaporates completely.
8. Pour in red wine and cook until the wine has evaporated.
9. Stir in chopped tomatoes, beef stock, and thyme. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer covered for 1.5 hours, adding more stock if needed. Season with salt and pepper.
10. Stir in Worcestershire sauce and cook for an additional 10 minutes. Remove from heat.
11. Prepare the potatoes: peel, cut, and mash the potatoes with butter, milk, salt, pepper, and nutmeg until smooth.
12. Place the cooked meat mixture into a baking dish.
13. Spread the mashed potatoes evenly over the top of the meat.
14. Brush the egg yolk evenly over the potato layer.
15. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for 30 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and crusty.
